subject: Science
description: know that an element is a pure substance represented by a chemical symbol and that a compound is a pure substance represented by a chemical formula

subject: Science
description: recognize that a limited number of elements make up the largest portion of solid Earth, living matter, oceans, and the atmosphere

subject: Science
description: differentiate between elements and compounds on the most basic level

subject: Science
description: compare metals, nonmetals, and metalloids using physical properties such as luster, conductivity, and malleability

subject: Science
description: calculate density to identify an unknown substance

subject: Science
description: compare and contrast potential and kinetic energy

subject: Science
description: identify and describe the changes in position, direction, and speed of an object when acted upon by unbalanced forces

subject: Science
description: investigate methods of thermal energy transfer, including conduction, convection, and radiation

subject: Science
description: verify through investigations that thermal energy moves in a predictable pattern from warmer to cooler until all substances attain the same temperature

subject: Science
description: build a model to illustrate the structural layers of Earth, including the inner core, outer core, mantle, crust, asthenosphere, and lithosphere

subject: Science
description: classify rocks as metamorphic, igneous, or sedimentary by the processes of their formation

subject: Science
description: identify the major tectonic plates, including Eurasian, African, Indo-Australian, Pacific, North American, and South American

subject: Science
description: describe the interactions between solar, weather, and ocean systems

subject: Science
description: understand that all organisms are composed of one or more cells

subject: Science
description: recognize that the presence of a nucleus is a key factor used to determine whether a cell is prokaryotic or eukaryotic

subject: Science
description: recognize that the broadest taxonomic classification of living organisms is divided into currently recognized domains

subject: Science
description: identify the basic characteristics of organisms, including prokaryotic and eukaryotic, unicellular and multicellular, autotroph and heterotroph, and aerobic and anaerobic

subject: Science
description: recognize that radiant energy from the Sun is transformed into chemical energy through the process of photosynthesis

subject: Science
description: diagram the flow of energy through living systems, including food chains, food webs, and energy pyramids

subject: Science
description: identify that organic compounds contain carbon and other elements such as hydrogen, oxygen, phosphorus, nitrogen, or sulfur

subject: Science
description: distinguish between physical and chemical changes in matter

subject: Science
description: contrast situations where work is done with different amounts of force to situations where no work is done such as holding a book at a constant height, pushing against a wall, or pulling a wagon

subject: Science
description: illustrate the transformation of energy within an organism such as the transfer from chemical energy to heat and thermal energy in digestion

subject: Science
description: predict and describe how catastrophic events such as floods, hurricanes, or tornadoes impact ecosystems

subject: Science
description: analyze the effects of weathering, erosion, and deposition on the environment in ecoregions of Texas

subject: Science
description: analyze the characteristics of objects in our solar system that allow life to exist such as the proximity of the Sun, presence of water, and composition of the atmosphere

subject: Science
description: identify the accommodations, considering the characteristics of our solar system, that enabled manned space exploration

subject: Science
description: observe and describe how different environments, including microhabitats in schoolyards and biomes, support different varieties of organisms

subject: Science
description: describe how biodiversity contributes to the sustainability of an ecosystem

subject: Science
description: examine organisms or their structures such as insects or leaves and use dichotomous keys for identification

subject: Science
description: explain variation within a population or species by comparing external features, behaviors, or physiology of organisms that enhance their survival

subject: Science
description: investigate and explain how internal structures of organisms have adaptations that allow specific functions such as gills in fish, hollow bones in birds, or xylem in plants

subject: Science
description: identify the main functions of the systems of the human organism, including the circulatory, respiratory, skeletal, muscular, digestive, excretory, reproductive, integumentary, nervous, and endocrine systems

subject: Science
description: investigate how organisms respond to external stimuli found in the environment such as phototropism and fight or flight

subject: Science
description: define heredity as the passage of genetic instructions from one generation to the next generation

subject: Science
description: describe the structure of atoms, including the masses, electrical charges, and locations of protons and neutrons in the nucleus and electrons in the electron cloud

subject: Science
description: identify that protons determine an element's identity and valence electrons determine its chemical properties, including reactivity

subject: Science
description: interpret the arrangement of the Periodic Table, including groups and periods, to explain how properties are used to classify elements

subject: Science
description: recognize that chemical formulas are used to identify substances and determine the number of atoms of each element in chemical formulas containing subscripts

subject: Science
description: investigate how evidence of chemical reactions indicates that new substances with different properties are formed

subject: Science
description: recognize whether a chemical equation containing coefficients is balanced or not and how that relates to the law of conservation of mass

subject: Science
description: demonstrate and calculate how unbalanced forces change the speed or direction of an object's motion

subject: Science
description: differentiate between speed, velocity, and acceleration

subject: Science
description: investigate and describe applications of Newton's three laws of motion

subject: Science
description: investigate and identify how the amount of force can affect the rate at which energy is transformed

subject: Science
description: describe components of the universe, including stars, nebulae, and galaxies, and use models such as the Hertzsprung-Russell diagram for classification

subject: Science
description: recognize that the Sun is a medium-sized star near the edge of a disc-shaped galaxy of stars and that the Sun is many thousands of times closer to Earth than any other star

subject: Science
description: explore how different wavelengths of the electromagnetic spectrum such as light and radio waves are used to gain information about distances and properties of components in the universe

subject: Science
description: describe the role of gravity in the formation of our solar system

subject: Science
description: recognize that the Sun provides the energy that drives convection within the atmosphere and oceans, producing winds and ocean currents

subject: Science
description: identify how global patterns of atmospheric movement influence local weather using weather maps that show high and low pressures and fronts

subject: Science
description: describe the interactions that occur among systems that perform the functions of regulation, nutrient absorption, reproduction, and defense from injury or illness in animals

subject: Science
description: describe the interactions that occur among systems that perform the functions of transport, reproduction, and response in plants

subject: Science
description: recognize levels of organization in plants and animals, including cells, tissues, organs, organ systems, and organisms

subject: Science
description: investigate and explain how organisms, including humans, and ecosystems interact and are dependent upon and may be impacted by both biotic and abiotic factors
